purpose: Map the user-supplied LG 9-company IR package into the common source-to-claim harness without letting company-specific download patterns change downstream schemas.

sourcePackages.name: company identity and market identifiers
sourcePackages.lgUse: Confirm DART/KRX/IR routing and aliases for all nine LG listed affiliates in the supplied package.

sourcePackages.name: financial disclosure baseline
sourcePackages.lgUse: Support DART-backed financial baseline and official report context.

sourcePackages.name: earnings and investor presentation baseline
sourcePackages.lgUse: Support investor-facing brief cards and narrative source-backed claims from earnings materials.

sourcePackages.name: shareholder value and capital action baseline
sourcePackages.lgUse: Support value-up, dividend, buyback, capex, and capital-allocation claims only when source-backed.

sourcePackages.name: business pipeline and segment strategy baseline
sourcePackages.lgUse: Support appliance, vehicle components, OLED, battery, cathode/materials, telecom, DX/cloud, and holding-company themes only after promotion.

sourcePackages.name: governance and disclosure baseline
sourcePackages.lgUse: Use AGM and governance materials when governance or disclosure-control questions enter frozen scenarios.

downloadAdapterNotes: LG Electronics, LG Energy Solution, LG H&H, LG CNS, and most LG Corp. files have direct public document URLs.
LG Chem direct files may require an official-site Referer header at collection time, but the artifact still records document-level URLs.
LG Display uses session/S3-style attachment URLs; these remain document-level URLs but may need refresh if stale.
LG Innotek uses POST-style file IDs; the adapter records constructed download endpoints and keeps the source page as the citable route.
LG Uplus uses dynamic browser downloads; rows without document-level URLs remain source-page-only until exact document URLs are supplied.
acceptanceChecklist: Each company has at least one company-scoped folder and identifier-verified companyId.
Each source has a document-level URL, constructed official file endpoint, source-page URL, or explicit reason why only local-file evidence exists.
Each source has a one-line selection reason.
PDF files are validated by file header before extraction.
XLSX files remain conversion/manual-review candidates, not runtime knowledge.
Session-based or dynamic download records are flagged without blocking local extraction.
Runtime promotion requires exact evidence locator, company scope, forward-looking label where needed, and reviewed source-backed claim text.
runtimePolicy: The supplied LG source package is review evidence. No LG narrative source is runtime-eligible until promoted into source-backed claims with companyId, companyScope, evidence locator, and rights-safe source metadata.
